MEADOW VIEW REHABILITATION & HEALTHCARE CENTER

CCN: 395092 · MONTROSE, PA 18801 · Susquehanna County

Overview

Address
225 PARK STREET, MONTROSE, PA 18801
Phone
5702783836
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
57 (90% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1967-01-01
Setting
Rural
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.85 3.88 2.83 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.60 0.68 0.59 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.56 0.86 0.55 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.69 2.34 1.69 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.15 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.61
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.41
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.60
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
44.2%
annual
RN turnover
45.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.37
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.00
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-04 9 5 4 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-11-07 12 8 8 72 2 108
Total weighted health score
63.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (31)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

30 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-09-04 2025-10-02
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-09-04 2025-10-02
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-09-04 2025-10-02
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-09-04 2025-10-02
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-09-04 2025-10-02
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-13 2025-05-27
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-03-19 2025-05-13
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-03-19 2025-05-13
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-03-19 2025-05-13
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. E 2024-11-07 2024-12-10
0574 The resident has the right to receive notices in a format and a language he or she understands. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-08
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-08
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-08
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-08
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-12-14 2024-05-08
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2023-12-14 2024-05-08
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-12-14 2024-03-15
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. E 2023-12-14 2024-03-15
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2023-12-14 2024-02-07
